key_facts: GNC Holdings LLC formed Oct 2020 via acquisition from bankrupt General Nutrition Corporation
3 operating segments: US & Canada, International, Wholesale
Operates company-owned stores, franchised stores, e-commerce, wholesale
Revenue recognition: retail sales at point of sale; e-commerce upon shipment; franchise fees deferred over license term; royalties recognized when franchisee sales occur
Inventory: FIFO, lower of cost or NRV, finished product only
PP&E depreciation: straight-line; building 30yr, machinery 3-10yr, leasehold 1-15yr, furniture 5-8yr, software 3-5yr
Goodwill: tested annually Q4, no impairment recorded 2022-2024
Brand name ($97M): indefinite-lived, tested annually, no impairment 2022-2024
Long-lived asset impairments 2024: $23.1M ($14.0M underperforming stores, $9.1M Rite Aid relationship intangible)
Leases: all operating leases, weighted avg remaining term 5.3 years, weighted avg discount rate 8.1%
Self-insurance for workers' comp and general liability with stop-loss
Income taxes: asset and liability method; valuation allowance assessed
Vendor allowances: $43.5M (2024), $41.2M (2023), $33.8M (2022) reduction to COGS
LIBOR to SOFR transition completed Q2 2023
US&Canada segment FY2024: company-owned $815.8M, wholesale to franchisees $144.1M, royalties/fees $21.7M, co-op advertising $13.5M, other $37.1M = $1,032.2M
International segment FY2024: wholesale to franchisees $67.4M, royalties $15.8M, other $14.6M = $97.8M
Wholesale segment FY2024: $44.6M (down from $84.3M in 2022)
US revenue $1,150.5M (98%), Foreign $24.1M (2%)
Franchise fees deferred: $7.6M contract liability at YE2024
PRO Access + loyalty: $12.0M deferred at YE2024
Gift card liability: $2.3M at YE2024
Income tax expense $26.5M in a loss year — driven by valuation allowance and state taxes
Deferred tax details in financial notes — complex; includes NOL carryforwards
Goodwill $104.4M unchanged 2023-2024 (all in US&Canada segment)
Brand name $97.0M (indefinite-lived, unchanged)
Other intangibles net: $71.9M (down from $87.4M) — includes franchise agreements, wholesale relationships, customer lists
Rite Aid wholesale relationship: $9.1M impairment in 2024
PP&E net: $47.9M (down from $62.9M)
Store-level impairments: $14.0M in 2024 for underperforming corporate stores
Capitalized software: $28.0M impairment in 2024 for terminated technology projects
Initial Term Loan (Bank of China): $205.4M outstanding; due $20M May 2025, balance Oct 2025; rate SOFR + 5.25% (as of Jan 2025); total rate was 9.43% in 2024
Second Lien Term Loan: $149.5M; maturity Oct 2026; rate SOFR + 9.75%; total rate 14.73% in 2024
Shareholder Loan (Jacks Management): $20.0M; due April 2025; used to satisfy 1L Term Loan amortization
Financial covenants: max leverage 3.5:1 (Dec 2024), 3.0:1 thereafter; min DSCR 1.25:1; guarantee EBITDA coverage 90%; guarantee asset coverage 90%
Company was NOT required to report on covenant compliance as of Dec 31, 2024 or June 30, 2025 per agreement with Bank of China
Debt issuance costs: $3.3M remaining
HK JV and China JV: 35% ownership each
Revenue from JVs: $5.4M (2024)
Receivables from JVs: $2.0M (2024, down from $17.0M in 2023)
Manufacturing JV: dissolved Q2 2023; $5.7M gain on dissolution in 2023
Deferred revenue and other current liabilities: $44.3M (2024)
Includes franchise fees $7.6M, loyalty program $12.0M, gift cards $2.3M
All operating leases; remaining terms 1-13 years (extensions up to 15 years)
Weighted avg remaining term: 5.3 years (2024)
Weighted avg discount rate: 8.1% (2024)
Lease liabilities: $153.5M ($43.9M current + $109.7M LT)
ROU assets: $135.7M
Sublease income: $1.5M (2024) from franchisee subleases
ROU impairments: $8.7M (2024)
Cash paid for leases: $63.8M (2024)
Purchase commitments: $17.0M as of YE2024
Singapore/Philippines arbitration award: $44.7M + $8.7M fees — contingent gain, not recognized
Environmental: no material liability expected
ZT Biopharmaceutical: $3.0M annual management fee; $8.0M guarantee fee ($1.5M paid, $2.0M written off); total payable $13.0M
Jacks Management: $20.0M shareholder loan (repaid Q2 2025)
HK JV/China JV: 35% owned; $2.0M receivable; $5.4M revenue from JVs
Total related party payables: $13.0M (all current at YE2024)
Q2 2025: Repaid $20.9M shareholder loan (principal + interest) to Jacks Management
Q2 2025: Paid $20.6M to Bank of China ($20M amortization + $0.6M ECF)
Q2 2025: Entered Revolving Loan with Bank of East Asia for up to CNY 1.5B for working capital
Financial covenants waived for Dec 31, 2024 and June 30, 2025 test dates
